steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage builds to a logical conclusion: observational data can't constrain Titan's methane mole fraction range, so the IPSL model's developers responded by prescribing a single uniform value. The blank must follow from that fact.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "further observations of Titan may clarify the moon's methane mole fraction sufficiently for the model to employ a single value rather than a range.": reverses the logic — the passage says sparse data forced a uniform value, not that better data would justify one. ✗
  • "some disagreements between the model's simulations of Titan's precipitation and humidity and the moon's actual precipitation and humidity are to be expected.": directly follows from the setup. A uniform methane value can't capture real variation, so model–reality disagreements are predictable. ✓
  • "inconsistencies across the model's simulations of Titan's precipitation and humidity could be attributable to variations in the moon's methane mole fraction.": talks about internal inconsistencies within the model's own outputs, which the passage does not raise. ✗
  • "even though the model's outputs sometimes agree with observational data, Titan's real methane mole fraction is likely higher than the methane mole fraction used in the model.": speculates about which direction the real value differs — nothing in the passage supports this. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
